epigraph.quote: Old, old, village, steep pedestrian streets, water for horses to drink, central gutters down the streets, lush gardens.

moments.quote: We stopped in at a camping stop near Lichfield. There were several electric power stations on the River Trent
We had lunch in the queues on the quayside, & had to keep interrupting to move up.

images.caption: Sketched by Eve while Simon in supermarket

moments.quote: [Route sketch of France, labelled 1992, with route line showing:] Le Havre / Vallée de la Seine / Châteaux de la Loire / Bourges / Périgord Caves / Gorges de Truyères / Cassaniouze / Conques
Once off the boat we motored for ½ - 1 hr. to a small campsite in a village, ending up in the dark by mistake in the residential end.
No choice, just plonked in front of you, so much easier with the children.
There is a lovely facade & bas relief, light & feathery.
lovely city walls, still lived in in higgledy piggledy houses + lush gardens in the old moat.
the boys had pigeon & we, Simon & I, had duck.
balustraded walks along the wide river, trout old houses.
The caves were wonderful, 'grabbing', scraped drawings of mammoths, or black charcoal.
A superb view of green hillside & valley & one red-tiled roof.

images.caption: Sketched by Eve while Simon in supermarket

moments.quote: There is a black madonna, enthroned, with a regal child on her lap.

moments.place: Camping Le Lot, near Rocamadour, France
moments.quote: It had a small swimming pool & a lovely old stone barn.
One fish-eater, skimmed across a pool to scoop up a fish. Others swooped in front of us from tower to tower.
The concert was marvellous in the floodlit nave.
John grew more & more confident swimming out from the chute. We all had an ice cream afterwards to celebrate his swimming the width.
[Route sketch of France, labelled 1992, with route line showing:] ... Châteaux de la Loire / Bourges / Périgord Caves ...
Hotel du Parc, was the best, most friendly of the holiday.

images.caption: Pool in confined area at campsite

moments.quote: Thomas + Eve spent an hour looking at stained-glass windows starting to devise a quiz for the B'ham cathedral choir trip in October.
Ten or so children sit around the edge of it, while each child in turn has a bounce in the middle.
On the boat we just had sandwiches + watched Sean Connery in "Medicine Man".
After Southampton we called in on Hathie, but unfortunately they were all out. There were awful traffic jams.
